i: MIREMPET (Ministério dos Recursos Minerais, Petróleo e Gás) — Angola's ministry overseeing oil and gas operations, licensing, and regulation. ANPG (Agência Nacional de Petróleo, Gás e Biocombustíveis) — the national petroleum regulator, created in 2019 to replace Sonangol's dual role as regulator and operator. Lei 3/20 (Law on Public Probity) — Angola's anti-corruption law establishing asset declaration requirements and conflicts of interest rules for public officials. Whistleblower protection remains weak.

Sonangol E.P. — Angola's state oil company and the country's largest enterprise. Sonangol holds equity in all oil production sharing agreements and manages the state's petroleum interests. The company underwent restructuring in 2019, separating its regulatory functions to ANPG. TotalEnergies, BP, ExxonMobil, Eni — major international oil companies operating deepwater blocks offshore Angola.

ANPG (Agência Nacional de Petróleo, Gás e Biocombustíveis) — established in 2019 as the independent petroleum regulator, responsible for managing exploration and production contracts, monitoring compliance with license terms, and administering bidding rounds. Ministério do Ambiente — Angola's environment ministry, responsible for 環境 policy, impact assessments, and pollution control.

Assembleia Nacional (National Assembly of Angola) — unicameral legislature with 220 members. Angola's electoral system uses party-list proportional representation. The most recent elections were held in 2022. Citizens can engage through assembleia.ao. Angola is a presidential republic with significant executive power. EITI — Angola was suspended from EITI in 2017 for insufficient progress but successfully reapplied and was readmitted in 2020, committing to enhanced 透明性 standards.

AR.s: G20 経済 ($638.4B GDP) with massive Vaca Muerta shale formation (second-largest shale gas, fourth-largest shale oil reserves globally). Agricultural powerhouse (soy, beef) with 5.8% ag GDP share but outsized 環境 footprint. Chronic macroeconomic instability.

sectors.w: Argentina's Vaca Muerta development requires massive capital investment ($10B+/year) and government subsidies (Plan Gas). Removing subsidies breaks the 重複利益 — without price guarantees, many Vaca Muerta wells are marginal at global market prices.
bp: Phase out Plan Gas subsidies and redirect capital to 再生可能エネルギー. Argentina has world-class wind resources in Patagonia and solar in the northwest. ...の場合 renewable electricity undercuts subsidized gas, the 重複利益 between producers and government in continued fossil expansion breaks.

Mandatory per-well 排出 disclosure including methane venting and flaring. Argentina's Secretaría de Energía already collects production data — extending to 排出 makes W computable from production statistics.
破壊不可能 by policy alone. Shale gas extraction and combustion release CO₂ and methane by physical process.
a: Report 環境 violations at Vaca Muerta operations to the provincial 環境当局 (Secretaría de Ambiente de Neuquén)
Document water contamination from fracking operations — report to AIC (Autoridad Interjurisdiccional de las Cuencas)
Report safety violations to the Secretaría de Trabajo

実施する TCFD-aligned climate disclosure across all operations
に投資する CCUS (carbon capture, utilization, and storage) pilot projects for gas processing facilities

i: Secretaría de Ambiente de Neuquén — Neuquén province's 環境当局, responsible for 環境 permitting and monitoring of oil and gas operations in the Vaca Muerta formation. AIC (Autoridad Interjurisdiccional de las Cuencas de los ríos Limay, Neuquén y Negro) — the inter-jurisdictional water authority managing the river basins affected by Vaca Muerta operations. COFEMA (Consejo Federal de Medio Ambiente) — federal-provincial 環境 coordination body. Argentina lacks comprehensive federal 内部告発者 protection legislation, though provincial laws vary.

YPF S.A. — Argentina's largest energy company (51% 国有), the dominant operator in Vaca Muerta. YPF's strategic decisions on Vaca Muerta development drive national energy policy. Pan American Energy, Vista Energy, TotalEnergies — other major operators in Vaca Muerta. Plan Gas.Ar — the Argentine government's gas production incentive program, guaranteeing minimum prices for domestic gas production to stimulate Vaca Muerta development.

Secretaría de Energía — Argentina's energy secretariat, responsible for energy policy, production monitoring, and regulatory oversight. SAyDS (Secretaría de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sustentable) — the federal environment secretariat, responsible for 環境 policy and 気候変動. ENARGAS (Ente Nacional Regulador del Gas) — the national gas regulatory authority, overseeing gas transport, distribution, and tariff regulation.

Congreso de la Nación Argentina — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (257 members) and the Senado (72 members). Citizens can contact representatives through diputados.gov.ar and senado.gob.ar. The Comisión de Energía y Combustibles handles energy legislation. Argentina's NDC — Argentina submitted an updated NDC in 2021 targeting 排出 of no more than 349 MtCO₂e by 2030. Climate Action Tracker rates this as 'highly insufficient.'

AT.s: Wealthy EU 経済 ($534.8B GDP) with strong manufacturing (15.1% of GDP). Low direct fossil fuel exposure but significant UPF and financial sector PST. Major transit hub for Russian gas (Baumgarten hub). High healthcare expenditure (11.83% GDP) suggests diet-related disease burden.

sectors.w: Austria's healthcare system (ASVG social insurance) tracks diet-related disease costs with high granularity. ...の場合 per-product-category healthcare costs are published alongside retail revenue, Wが計算可能になる from bilateral UPF transactions.
bp: 実施する front-of-pack health-impact labelling through EU Farm to Fork Strategy. ...の場合 consumers see welfare costs at point of sale, the 重複利益 between UPF manufacturers and retailers in high-margin processed products 弱まる.

Mandate publication of diet-attributable healthcare costs per product category using ASVG social insurance data. Austria's comprehensive health insurance system generates the data needed to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix in ways that affect metabolic response. 改革するulation mandates can reduce but not eliminate システム依存性.

Support EU-wide adoption of front-of-pack nutritional labelling
Advocate for inclusion of diet-attributable health costs in national accounting
を推進する： WHO-aligned UPF marketing restrictions through EU legislative process
i: Bundeswettbewerbsbehörde (BWB / Federal Competition Authority) — Austria's competition authority, investigating unfair commercial practices including misleading advertising. AGES (Agentur für Gesundheit und Ernährungssicherheit / Austrian Agency for Health and Food Safety) — the federal agency responsible for food safety, nutrition surveillance, and public health. AGES conducts food inspections and risk assessments. HinweisgeberInnenschutzgesetz (HSchG, 2023) — Austria's transposition of the EU Whistleblower Directive, providing protection for employees reporting violations of EU or national law.

EU Farm to Fork Strategy — includes proposals for mandatory front-of-pack nutrition labelling across the EU. Austrian food companies that move early gain competitive advantage in the EU single market. EFSA (European Food Safety Authority) — provides scientific opinions that shape EU food regulation applicable in Austria.

BMSGPK (Bundesministerium für Soziales, Gesundheit, Pflege und Konsumentenschutz / Federal Ministry of Social Affairs, Health, Care and Consumer Protection) — oversees food safety policy, nutrition guidelines, and consumer protection. Hauptverband der österreichischen Sozialversicherungsträger (now Dachverband) — the umbrella organization of Austrian social insurance institutions, managing healthcare data for the entire insured population.

Österreichisches Parlament — Austria's bicameral parliament consisting of the Nationalrat (183 members) and the Bundesrat (61 members). Citizens can contact representatives through parlament.gv.at. The Gesundheitsausschuss (Health Committee) handles health and food policy legislation.

AZ.s: Oil-dependent 経済 with 29.57% combined oil and gas rents ($22B). SOCAR (State Oil Company) dominates. Hosted COP29 (2024). Authoritarian governance with limited 市民社会 space. Caspian Sea oil and gas fields (ACG, Shah Deniz) are mature.

sectors.w: Azerbaijan's oil production is declining from mature Caspian fields (ACG peaked in 2010). As production costs rise and reserves deplete, the 重複利益 between SOCAR and international partners (BP) 弱まる — diversification becomes economically necessary.
bp: Accelerate post-oil diversification using SOFAZ (State Oil Fund) reserves. Azerbaijan's $50B+ sovereign wealth fund can finance 再生可能エネルギー and non-oil economic development. ...の場合 SOFAZ investment returns from renewables exceed declining oil returns, the 重複利益 breaks.
実施する 油田ごとの 排出 disclosure for all Caspian operations. BP already publishes 排出 data for ACG — extending to all SOCAR operations makes W computable across the sector.
破壊不可能 by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report 環境 violations at oil facilities to the Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources
Document 排出 underreporting through internal SOCAR channels
Report 環境 concerns to international partners (BP) through their compliance systems
SOCAR: 策定する binding 再生可能エネルギー transition plan with interim 排出 targets
BP Azerbaijan: Extend ACG 排出 透明性 standards to all partner operations
SOFAZ: 採用する ESG investment criteria excluding new fossil fuel development
Ministry of Ecology: 強化する 環境 standards for offshore Caspian operations

Tariff Council: 改革する energy pricing to remove implicit fossil fuel consumption subsidies
Leverage Azerbaijan's COP29 presidency legacy to strengthen national climate commitments

Support EITI enhanced reporting with 環境 metrics
を推進する： European Energy Charter Treaty enforcement on 環境 standards in transit countries
i: Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources — Azerbaijan's 環境当局, responsible for 環境 monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. The Ministry oversees 環境 impact assessments for energy projects. SOCAR (State Oil Company of the Azerbaijan Republic) — the national oil company controlling all upstream and downstream petroleum operations. Note: Azerbaijan has no comprehensive 内部告発者 protection law. Civil society space is severely constrained; independent media and NGOs face significant operational barriers.

SOCAR — Azerbaijan's state oil company, operating the country's refining, petrochemical, and upstream assets. SOCAR also has international operations in Turkey, Georgia, and Europe. BP — the largest international operator in Azerbaijan, managing the Azeri-Chirag-Deepwater Gunashli (ACG) oilfield and Shah Deniz gas field through Production Sharing Agreements. SOFAZ (State Oil Fund of the Republic of Azerbaijan) — the sovereign wealth fund managing Azerbaijan's petroleum revenues, with assets exceeding $50B.

Azerbaijan EITI — Azerbaijan was an early EITI member (2003) and publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues. The EITI process provides one of the few independent 透明性 mechanisms in the sector. Tariff (Price) Council — sets regulated energy prices in Azerbaijan. Tehran Convention — the Framework Convention for the Protection of the Marine Environment of the Caspian Sea, which governs 環境 standards for offshore operations.

Milli Majlis (National Assembly of Azerbaijan) — unicameral parliament with 125 members. Note: Azerbaijan is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House; elections are not considered free and fair by international observers. Civic engagement channels are limited. International pressure operates primarily through multilateral forums including the Council of Europe (Azerbaijan is a member), OSCE, and the EITI Board. The EU-Azerbaijan Partnership and Cooperation Agreement provides a framework for dialogue on energy and 環境 issues.

BA.s: Post-conflict 経済 ($29.6B GDP) with complex governance (two entities: Federation and Republika Srpska). Coal-dependent energy sector and manufacturing (12.7% GDP). EU加盟候補国 country. Significant coal 鉱業 and thermal power generation.

sectors.w: Bosnia's coal sector is heavily subsidized through below-market electricity pricing and state employment guarantees. EU accession requires alignment with the EU ETS — when carbon pricing applies, the 重複利益 between coal producers and the state employment system breaks.
bp: EU accession alignment with EU ETS carbon pricing. ...の場合 Bosnian coal power must pay the carbon price (~€90/ton), coal-fired electricity becomes uncompetitive with imported renewables. The 重複利益 between mine operators and state employment programs breaks.
Mandatory 排出 monitoring and disclosure per power plant. Energy Community Treaty membership requires EU-aligned 環境 reporting — implementing this makes W computable from electricity generation data.
破壊不可能. Coal combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, and particulate matter by physical process.
a: Report 環境 violations at coal mines and power plants to entity-level 環境 inspectorates
Document health impacts in communities near coal power plants — report to public health institutes
Report corruption in coal procurement to the Agency for Prevention of Corruption and Coordination of the Fight Against Corruption
State electricity companies (EPBiH, ERS): 策定する coal phase-out timelines aligned with Energy Community Treaty obligations
に投資する 再生可能エネルギー capacity to replace aging coal plants

DERK (State Electricity Regulatory Commission): 改革する tariffs to reflect true 環境 costs of coal generation
Entity 環境 agencies: 施行する air quality standards at coal power plants
Energy Community Secretariat: Use dispute resolution to enforce 環境 compliance

Support EU accession negotiations contingent on energy transition commitments
Advocate for just transition funding for coal-dependent communities through IPA III (EU Instrument for Pre-Accession)
を推進する： Energy Community Treaty enforcement on air quality standards
i: Environmental inspection is split between the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina and Republika Srpska, each maintaining separate 環境 agencies. Federation: Federalni inspektorat — 環境 inspectorate. Republika Srpska: Republička uprava za inspekcijske poslove — inspection administration. Agency for Prevention of Corruption and Coordination of the Fight Against Corruption (APIK) — state-level anti-corruption body. Bosnia lacks comprehensive 内部告発者 protection legislation, though draft laws have been discussed as part of EU accession reforms.

EPBiH (Elektroprivreda Bosne i Hercegovine) — the Federation's state electricity company, operating coal-fired power plants and hydropower. ERS (Elektroprivreda Republike Srpske) — Republika Srpska's state electricity company. Both companies operate aging coal infrastructure (many plants dating to Yugoslav era) and are major employers in 鉱業 communities.

DERK (Državna regulatorna komisija za električnu energiju / State Electricity Regulatory Commission) — the state-level electricity regulator. FERK and RERS are entity-level regulators. Energy Community Treaty — Bosnia is a contracting party, committed to implementing EU energy and 環境 acquis including Large Combustion Plants Directive and Industrial Emissions Directive.

Parlamentarna skupština Bosne i Hercegovine (Parliamentary Assembly) — bicameral legislature consisting of the Predstavnički dom (House of Representatives, 42 members) and the Dom naroda (House of Peoples, 15 members). Bosnia's complex governance structure (Dayton Agreement) divides power between state, entity, and cantonal levels. Citizens can contact representatives through parlament.ba.

BD.s: Large 経済 ($450.1B GDP) driven by garment manufacturing (21.9% mfg share of GDP). High agricultural employment (11.2% GDP). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying delta at severe flood and cyclone risk. One of the world's most densely populated countries.

sectors.w: Bangladesh's rice monoculture depletes groundwater (arsenic contamination) and reduces soil biodiversity. Satellite-based crop monitoring and water quality data from BWDB can make W computable from agricultural output and water extraction volumes.
bp: 改革する agricultural subsidies from input-based (fertilizer, diesel) to outcome-based (soil health, water efficiency). ...の場合 subsidies reward diversification rather than rice intensification, ...における重複利益 monoculture between farmers and input suppliers 弱まる.

Mandatory groundwater extraction monitoring and arsenic contamination mapping published at upazila level. BWDB already collects water data — extending to public disclosure linked to agricultural output makes W computable.
部分的に破壊可能. Intensive rice cultivation in deltaic conditions inherently alters hydrology and nutrient cycling. However, System of Rice Intensification (SRI) and crop diversification can reduce システム依存性.

Support Bangladesh's Climate Vulnerable Forum leadership in advocating for agricultural adaptation finance

i: DAE (Department of Agricultural Extension) — the primary government agency for agricultural technical support and extension services, operating at union, upazila, and district levels. DPHE (Department of Public Health Engineering) — responsible for water supply and sanitation, including monitoring arsenic contamination in groundwater (affecting ~35 million people). BFSA (Bangladesh Food Safety Authority) — established in 2015 to regulate food safety across the supply chain. Bangladesh lacks comprehensive 内部告発者 protection legislation.

BRRI (Bangladesh Rice Research Institute) — the national research institute developing improved rice varieties, including flood-tolerant (BRRI dhan52), salt-tolerant, and drought-resistant varieties. BARI (Bangladesh Agricultural Research Institute) — conducts research on non-rice crops including vegetables, pulses, and oilseeds. BADC (Bangladesh Agricultural 策定するment Corporation) — the government agency responsible for seed production and distribution.

Ministry of Agriculture — oversees agricultural policy, subsidies, and development programs. BWDB (Bangladesh Water 策定するment Board) — responsible for flood control, drainage, and irrigation infrastructure. The BWDB manages the country's water resource data system. DoE (Department of Environment) — Bangladesh's 環境 regulatory agency, responsible for 環境 clearance of projects and monitoring pollution.

Jatiya Sangsad (National Parliament of Bangladesh) — unicameral legislature with 350 members (300 directly elected, 50 reserved for women). Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.gov.bd. The Standing Committee on Ministry of Agriculture handles agricultural policy. Bangladesh Climate Change Strategy and Action Plan (BCCSAP) — the national framework for climate adaptation and mitigation, including agricultural resilience.

BE.s: EU and NATO headquarters 経済 ($671.4B GDP). Services-dominant (71% GDP). Major port (Antwerp — Europe's second-largest) handles significant fossil fuel and petrochemical throughput. High healthcare expenditure (10.8% GDP). Federal state with three regions.

sectors.w: Belgium's comprehensive INAMI/RIZIV health insurance system tracks healthcare costs with high granularity. Publishing diet-attributable disease costs per product category makes W computable from retail sales data.
bp: 実施する front-of-pack health labelling (Belgium adopted Nutri-Score voluntarily in 2019). Extending to mandatory application and adding health-cost information 弱まる ...における重複利益 high-margin UPF.
INAMI/RIZIV publication of anonymized diet-attributable healthcare cost data per product category. Belgium's universal health insurance generates comprehensive data to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix affecting metabolic response. 改革するulation mandates reduce but do not eliminate システム依存性.

Document food safety violations — report to AFSCA/FAVV (Federal Agency for the Safety of the Food Chain)

i: AFSCA/FAVV (Agence fédérale pour la sécurité de la chaîne alimentaire / Federaal Agentschap voor de Veiligheid van de Voedselketen) — Belgium's federal food safety agency, responsible for food chain safety from farm to fork. Conducts inspections, sampling, and enforcement. SPF Économie (Service public fédéral Économie) — the federal economic affairs service handling consumer protection and fair commercial practices.

Fevia (Federation of the Belgian Food Industry) — the trade association representing over 700 food companies in Belgium. Fevia's sustainability commitments influence industry practices. Belgium hosts major food companies including Puratos, Vandemoortele, and Lotus Bakeries.

SPF Santé publique (Federal Public Service Health, Food Chain Safety and Environment) — the federal ministry overseeing public health policy, food safety, and environment. INAMI/RIZIV (Institut national d'assurance maladie-invalidité / Rijksinstituut voor ziekte- en invaliditeitsverzekering) — Belgium's national health insurance institute, managing the compulsory health insurance system and healthcare cost data.

Parlement fédéral / Federaal Parlement — Belgium's bicameral parliament consisting of the Chambre des représentants / Kamer van Volksvertegenwoordigers (150 members) and the Sénat / Senaat (60 members). Citizens can contact representatives through lachambre.be / dekamer.be. Belgium's federal structure means health policy is shared between federal and regional (community) governments.

BF.s: Low-income Sahelian 経済 ($23.1B GDP) with dominant gold 鉱業 sector (min=15.45% GDP). Agriculture employs 80%+ of population (20.9% GDP). Military junta since 2022 coup. Severe security challenges (jihadist insurgency).

sectors.w: Burkina Faso's gold 鉱業 generates cyanide contamination, mercury pollution (artisanal 鉱業), and community displacement. ...の場合 環境 and health costs per mine are published alongside production revenue, Wが計算可能になる.
bp: Mandate 環境 bonds and community benefit agreements for all industrial 鉱業 licenses. ...の場合 鉱業 companies must pre-fund 環境 remediation and community compensation, ...における重複利益 extraction without accountability 弱まる.

Publish 鉱山ごとの 環境 impact data (water quality, air quality, land disturbance) alongside production revenue through EITI reporting. Burkina Faso's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to 環境 data makes W visible.
部分的に破壊可能. Open-pit gold 鉱業 inherently disturbs landscapes and generates toxic waste. However, improved tailings management and mercury-free artisanal methods can reduce システム依存性.
a: Report 環境 violations at mine sites to BUNEE (Bureau National des Évaluations Environnementales)
Document community health impacts near 鉱業 operations through local health clinics
Report illegal artisanal 鉱業 activities and mercury use to provincial authorities
Mining companies (Endeavour Mining, IAMGOLD, Nordgold): 実施する Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management
採用する EITI enhanced reporting including 環境 impact metrics
に投資する mercury-free processing technologies for artisanal 鉱業 support programs
Ministry of Mines: 強化する 環境 conditions in 鉱業 permits and enforce compliance
BUMIGEB (Bureau des Mines et de la Géologie): Improve artisanal 鉱業 regulation to reduce mercury use
EITI-BF: Expand reporting to include 環境 and community impact data

Support African Mining Vision implementation for sustainable resource governance
を推進する： international 鉱業 companies to adopt binding 環境 standards through OECD Due Diligence Guidance
Support Kimberley Process-style certification for gold supply chains to reduce conflict mineral financing
i: BUNEE (Bureau National des Évaluations Environnementales) — Burkina Faso's national 環境 assessment authority, responsible for 環境 impact assessments of 鉱業 projects. Ministère de l'Environnement, de l'Énergie, de l'Eau et de l'Assainissement — oversees 環境 policy and regulation. Note: Under military rule since 2022, institutional capacity is constrained and 市民社会 space is restricted.

Major 鉱業 companies operating in Burkina Faso include Endeavour Mining (Houndé, Wahgnion mines), IAMGOLD (Essakane mine), and Nordgold (Bissa-Bouly). These international companies are subject to home-country ESG reporting requirements and international 鉱業 standards (ICMM, IFC Performance Standards).

Ministère des Mines et des Carrières — the ministry responsible for 鉱業 policy, licensing, and regulation. BUMIGEB (Bureau des Mines et de la Géologie du Burkina) — the geological survey and 鉱業 technical agency. EITI Burkina Faso — Burkina Faso has been an EITI member since 2009, publishing reports on 鉱業 revenues. The 2015 Mining Code establishes 環境 obligations for 鉱業 companies.

Note: Burkina Faso is under military rule (Capitaine Ibrahim Traoré since 2022). The Assemblée Législative de Transition (Transitional Legislative Assembly) replaced the elected National Assembly. Democratic institutions are suspended. International engagement channels: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States — though Burkina Faso announced withdrawal in 2024), African Union, and international development partners. The African Mining Vision (2009) provides a continental framework for sustainable 鉱業 governance.

BG.s: EU加盟国 経済 ($113.3B GDP) with legacy coal dependence (Maritsa East complex is one of Europe's largest lignite basins). Services-dominant (63.8% GDP). EU accession has driven institutional modernization but coal transition remains politically sensitive.

sectors.w: Bulgaria's coal sector is sustained by implicit subsidies (below-market electricity pricing, state employment). EU ETS carbon pricing makes lignite uncompetitive — when full carbon costs are internalized, the 重複利益 between mine operators and state employment programs breaks.
bp: Full alignment with EU ETS free allowance phase-out. ...の場合 Bulgarian lignite plants pay the EU carbon price without free allowances, coal-fired electricity becomes uncompetitive. The 重複利益 breaks when renewable alternatives are cheaper.

Mandatory 発電所ごとの 排出 and health-impact disclosure. Bulgaria's lignite plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) are among Europe's worst air polluters. Publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable from electricity generation data.
破壊不可能. Lignite combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, NOx, and particulate matter by physical process.

Ministry of Energy: 策定する binding coal phase-out timeline aligned with EU Green Deal

Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Stara Zagora and other coal-dependent regions
Advocate for accelerated EU ETS free allowance phase-out
を推進する： Territorial Just Transition Plans that include concrete retraining and economic diversification measures
i: RIOSV (Регионални инспекции по околната среда и водите / Regional Inspectorates of Environment and Water) — 16 regional 環境 agencies conducting inspections and enforcement. EEA (Изпълнителна агенция по околна среда / Executive Environment Agency) — the national 環境 monitoring agency collecting 排出 and air quality data. Bulgaria transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2023.

Maritsa East complex — comprises three lignite-fired power plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) and the associated Maritsa-Iztok mines. AES Bulgaria operates Maritsa East I; ContourGlobal operates Maritsa East III. The complex is the largest industrial employer in the Stara Zagora region. Mini Maritsa-Iztok — the 国有 lignite 鉱業 company supplying the Maritsa East plants.

KEVR (Комисия за енергийно и водно регулиране / Energy and Water Regulatory Commission) — Bulgaria's independent エネルギー規制当局, setting electricity tariffs and licensing. Ministry of Energy — oversees energy policy including the National Energy and Climate Plan (NECP). Bulgaria's NECP has been criticized for insufficient coal phase-out ambition.

Narodno Sabranie (Народно събрание / National Assembly of Bulgaria) — unicameral parliament with 240 members. Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.bg. The Комисия по енергетика (Energy Committee) handles energy legislation. EU Just Transition Fund — Bulgaria is eligible for significant JTF funding to support coal transition in affected regions. Bulgaria's Territorial Just Transition Plans cover Stara Zagora, Kyustendil, and Pernik regions.

BH.s: Small island petrostate ($47.1B GDP) with 16.64% combined oil and gas rents. Bahrain's Abu Safa offshore field (shared with Saudi Arabia) provides the majority of oil revenue. Diversifying into financial services (Bahrain Financial Harbour) and aluminum (Alba smelter).

sectors.w: Bahrain's oil reserves are the smallest among GCC states (declining production from mature fields). The economic case for diversification is strongest here — when post-oil planning makes renewable investment more attractive than marginal barrel extraction, the 重複利益 breaks.
bp: Accelerate Economic Vision 2030 diversification away from oil dependence. Bahrain's small size and advanced financial sector make it the most transition-ready GCC 経済. ...の場合 financial services and technology revenue exceeds oil revenue, ...における重複利益 extraction breaks.
Publish 油田ごとの 環境 and 排出 data for all Bahrain Petroleum Company (BAPCO) operations. Making lifecycle 排出 visible per barrel makes W computable from production data.
破壊不可能 by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

i: Supreme Council for Environment (SCE) — Bahrain's 環境当局, responsible for 環境 policy, permitting, and enforcement. SCE oversees 環境 impact assessments for industrial projects. BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — the 国有 oil company operating Bahrain's refinery and upstream assets. National Audit Office — conducts financial audits of government entities including BAPCO. Note: Bahrain has limited 内部告発者 protection; 市民社会 space is constrained.

BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — operates the Sitra refinery (267,000 bpd after modernization) and manages upstream production. Mumtalakat — Bahrain's sovereign wealth fund, managing state assets including BAPCO and Alba (Aluminium Bahrain). Nogaholding — the investment arm of Bahrain's National Oil and Gas Authority, overseeing the country's energy sector investments.

NOGA (National Oil and Gas Authority) — the government body responsible for oil and gas policy, licensing, and upstream regulation. CBB (Central Bank of Bahrain) — the financial sector regulator, which has begun integrating ESG considerations into banking supervision. Bahrain Economic 策定するment Board — coordinates the Economic Vision 2030 diversification strategy.

Council of Representatives (Majlis al-Nuwab) — the elected lower house of Bahrain's parliament (40 members) and the Shura Council (Majlis al-Shura, 40 appointed members). Note: Bahrain is a 立憲君主制 with significant royal authority. Political opposition is restricted. The Council of Representatives has limited legislative power. International engagement on climate issues operates through GCC coordination and UNFCCC processes.

BJ.s: Small West African 経済 ($21.5B GDP) with dominant agriculture sector (24.2% GDP, primarily cotton). Services growing (48.9% GDP). Cotton is the main export crop — Benin is Africa's largest cotton producer. Climate vulnerability through Sahel desertification and coastal erosion.

sectors.w: Benin's cotton monoculture degrades soil fertility and increases pesticide exposure. ...の場合 soil health data and pesticide-related healthcare costs are published alongside cotton production revenue, Wが計算可能になる from agricultural output data.
bp: 改革する cotton subsidies to reward soil health and crop diversification. ...の場合 input subsidies (seeds, fertilizer, pesticides) are redirected from cotton intensification to mixed farming systems, the 重複利益 between cotton traders and input suppliers 弱まる.
Mandatory publication of soil health metrics (organic matter, nutrient balance) and pesticide-related health costs per cotton-producing commune. Making 環境 and health costs visible makes W computable.

部分的に破壊可能. Cotton monoculture inherently depletes soil nutrients and requires pesticide application. However, integrated pest management and organic cotton production can reduce システム依存性 — Benin has a growing organic cotton sector.
a: Report pesticide misuse or adulteration to DPQC (Direction de la Promotion de la Qualité et du Conditionnement des Produits Agricoles)

i: DPQC (Direction de la Promotion de la Qualité et du Conditionnement des Produits Agricoles) — the government directorate responsible for agricultural product quality and standards. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — Benin's 環境 agency, responsible for 環境 impact assessment and monitoring. FONAC (Front des Organisations Nationales contre la Corruption) — a national anti-corruption coalition of 市民社会 organizations.

AIC (Association Interprofessionnelle du Coton) — the inter-professional cotton association coordinating relations between producers, ginners, and input suppliers. SODECO (Société de Développement du Coton) — the main cotton ginning company. The cotton sector was partially liberalized but remains state-coordinated through the AIC framework.

MAEP (Ministère de l'Agriculture, de l'Élevage et de la Pêche) — Benin's agriculture ministry, responsible for agricultural policy and cotton sector coordination. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — conducts 環境 monitoring and impact assessments. INSAE (Institut National de la Statistique et de l'Analyse Économique) — Benin's national statistics institute.

Assemblée Nationale du Bénin — unicameral parliament with 109 members. Citizens can contact their deputies through the official Assembly website. Note: Benin's democratic space has narrowed under recent constitutional reforms, though elections continue. Cotton-4 group — Benin, Burkina Faso, Chad, and Mali coordinate on cotton trade policy at the WTO, advocating against subsidies in developed countries that depress global cotton prices.

BN.s: Small wealthy petrostate ($15.3B GDP) with 24.23% combined oil and gas rents. Brunei Shell Petroleum (BSP, 50/50 JV with Shell) dominates the 経済. Absolute monarchy. Wawasan Brunei 2035 diversification strategy underway but oil/gas remain 60%+ of government revenue.

sectors.w: Brunei's oil production is in long-term decline (from 220,000 bpd peak to ~100,000 bpd). As reserves deplete, the 重複利益 between BSP and the government in continued extraction 弱まる — Wawasan 2035 diversification becomes economically necessary.
bp: Accelerate Wawasan 2035 economic diversification using Brunei Investment Agency (BIA) sovereign wealth. ...の場合 non-oil sectors generate more government revenue than declining oil production, ...における重複利益 extraction breaks.
実施する 油田ごとの 排出 disclosure for all BSP and Total operations. Shell's global ESG reporting standards can be extended to Brunei operations to make W computable.
破壊不可能 by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

Document 排出 data gaps — report to the Department of Environment, Parks and Recreation
Use BSP's internal reporting mechanisms for safety and 環境 concerns

i: BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — a 50/50 joint venture between the government of Brunei and Shell, the country's largest oil and gas producer. BSP operates onshore and offshore fields. Department of Environment, Parks and Recreation — under the Ministry of 策定するment, responsible for 環境 policy and regulation. Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy with no elected legislature. There is no 内部告発者 protection legislation. Reporting channels are primarily internal corporate mechanisms.

BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — managed jointly by the Brunei government and Shell. BIA (Brunei Investment Agency) — the sovereign wealth fund managing Brunei's petroleum revenues (estimated $30-40B). Total E&P Borneo — operates deep water gas fields offshore Brunei. PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority overseeing the sector.

PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority responsible for policy, regulation, and management of Brunei's oil and gas resources. AMBD (Autoriti Monetari Brunei Darussalam) — Brunei's central bank and financial regulator. Department of Energy — under the Prime Minister's Office, coordinates energy policy and the Wawasan 2035 energy diversification strategy.

Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy (Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah). There is no elected legislature — the Legislative Council (Majlis Mesyuarat Negara) is appointed and has advisory functions only. Civic engagement operates through petition to the Sultan and through ASEAN and international multilateral channels. ASEAN — Brunei is a founding member. ASEAN's Framework for Circular Economy and declaration on 気候変動 provide regional engagement mechanisms.

BO.s: Resource-dependent 経済 ($54.9B GDP) with significant natural gas reserves (gas=1.85% rents) and 鉱業 (min=5.91%, lithium in Salar de Uyuni — world's largest deposit). 農業がGDPの 8.8% GDP with soy expansion driving 森林破壊. Gas production declining from mature fields.

sectors.w: Bolivia holds the world's largest lithium reserves in the Salar de Uyuni. Lithium extraction uses massive water volumes in arid regions. ...の場合 water consumption and brine chemistry data are published alongside production revenue, Wが計算可能になる.
bp: Require community benefit agreements and water rights guarantees for all lithium extraction. ...の場合 indigenous communities (who hold constitutional land rights under Bolivia's 2009 Constitution) can negotiate fair terms, ...における重複利益 extraction without consent 弱まる.
Mandatory publication of water extraction volumes, brine chemistry changes, and downstream ecosystem data per lithium operation. Making 環境 costs visible makes W computable from production data.

部分的に破壊可能. Lithium brine extraction inherently alters aquifer chemistry and reduces water availability in arid ecosystems. However, direct lithium extraction (DLE) technology can significantly reduce water consumption compared to evaporation ponds.
a: Report 環境 violations at 鉱業 operations to the Autoridad de Fiscalización y Control Social de Bosques y Tierra (ABT)
Document water contamination or indigenous rights violations — report to the Defensoría del Pueblo

i: AJAM (Autoridad Jurisdiccional Administrativa Minera) — the 鉱業 administrative authority responsible for granting and overseeing 鉱業 concessions. Defensoría del Pueblo — Bolivia's human rights ombudsman, responsible for investigating complaints about government actions affecting fundamental rights, including indigenous and 環境 rights. Bolivia's 2009 Constitution (Article 30) — guarantees indigenous peoples' right to prior consultation on resource extraction in their territories.

YLB (Yacimientos de Litio Bolivianos) — Bolivia's state lithium company, managing lithium industrialization in the Salar de Uyuni. COMIBOL (Corporación Minera de Bolivia) — the state 鉱業 corporation overseeing Bolivia's 鉱業 sector. YPFB (Yacimientos Petrolíferos Fiscales Bolivianos) — the state hydrocarbons company.

Ministerio de Minería y Metalurgia — Bolivia's 鉱業 ministry, responsible for 鉱業 policy and regulation. MMAyA (Ministerio de Medio Ambiente y Agua) — the environment and water ministry, overseeing 環境 policy and water resource management. SENARECOM (Servicio Nacional de Registro y Control de la Comercialización de Minerales y Metales) — the national service for mineral commerce registration and control.

Asamblea Legislativa Plurinacional (Plurinational Legislative Assembly) — Bolivia's bicameral parliament consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (130 members) and the Cámara de Senadores (36 members). Bolivia's 2009 Constitution establishes a 'plurinational' state recognizing indigenous rights and communitarian 民主主義. ILO Convention 169 — Bolivia has ratified ILO Convention 169 on Indigenous and Tribal Peoples, requiring free, prior, and informed consultation on resource extraction in indigenous territories.

BS.s: Small island 経済 ($15.8B GDP) dominated by tourism and financial services (77.2% services). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying islands face existential risk from sea level rise and intensifying hurricanes (Hurricane Dorian, 2019, caused $3.4B damage — 25% of GDP).

sectors.w: The Bahamas' offshore financial center enables capital flows that may avoid tax obligations elsewhere. ...の場合 beneficial ownership information and tax 透明性 data are published, the system welfare impact of offshore finance becomes computable.
bp: 実施する full beneficial ownership 透明性 in line with FATF recommendations. ...の場合 the competitive advantage of Bahamian financial secrecy is eliminated through 透明性, the 重複利益 between offshore service providers and tax-avoiding clients 弱まる.

Publish aggregate data on capital flows, tax rulings, and beneficial ownership through enhanced OECD Common Reporting Standard participation. Making tax revenue impacts visible to source jurisdictions makes W computable.
部分的に破壊可能. Financial intermediation can relocate — but the Bahamas' geographic proximity to the US and established legal infrastructure create switching costs that give regulation leverage.

Document beneficial ownership concealment — report to the Securities Commission of The Bahamas

i: FIU (Financial Intelligence Unit of The Bahamas) — the agency responsible for receiving, analyzing, and disseminating suspicious transaction reports. The FIU works with law enforcement to combat money laundering and terrorist financing. Securities Commission of The Bahamas — regulates the investment funds industry and securities market. Central Bank of The Bahamas — the prudential regulator of banks and trust companies.

The Bahamas is home to approximately 200 licensed banks and trust companies. Major international banks maintain operations in Nassau. The Bahamas 金融サービス Board (BFSB) promotes the financial services sector and coordinates industry standards.

Central Bank of The Bahamas — the primary financial regulator and supervisor. The Compliance Commission — supervises non-bank financial service providers for anti-money laundering compliance. FATF (Financial Action Task Force) — The Bahamas has been subject to FATF enhanced monitoring and has implemented reforms to address identified deficiencies in its AML/CFT framework.

Parliament of The Bahamas — bicameral legislature consisting of the House of Assembly (39 members) and the Senate (16 appointed members). Citizens can contact their MP through the official government portal. The Bahamas is a Westminster-style 議院内閣制 民主主義. AOSIS (Alliance of Small Island States) — The Bahamas is an active member, advocating for climate action and sea level rise mitigation. CARICOM — provides regional coordination on financial regulation.

BW.s: Upper-middle-income 経済 ($19.4B GDP) with diamond 鉱業 historically driving growth (Debswana JV with De Beers). Services-dominant (63.5% GDP). One of Africa's most stable democracies. Diversification challenge as lab-grown diamonds threaten natural diamond demand.

sectors.w: Lab-grown diamonds are disrupting the natural diamond market (30%+ of gem market by volume). As synthetic alternatives become indistinguishable and cheaper, the 重複利益 between Debswana and consumers in natural diamond premium 弱まる — Botswana must diversify.
bp: Accelerate economic diversification using Pula Fund sovereign wealth. ...の場合 non-diamond revenue streams (tech, tourism, beef) exceed declining diamond revenues, ...における重複利益 continued diamond dependence breaks naturally.
Publish full lifecycle 環境 data (water use, land disturbance, energy consumption) per carat for all 鉱業 operations. Making 環境 costs visible makes W computable from diamond production data.
部分的に破壊可能. Open-pit diamond 鉱業 inherently disturbs landscapes. However, Botswana's mines are in relatively low-biodiversity Kalahari terrain, and rehabilitation programs can reduce long-term システム依存性.
a: Report 環境 violations at mine sites to DEA (Department of Environmental Affairs)
Document community displacement or inadequate compensation — report to the Ombudsman
Report safety violations to the Department of Mines

Support African Union Mining Vision implementation for sustainable diamond governance
Advocate for Kimberley Process reform to include 環境 and social standards
を推進する： SADC (Southern African 策定するment Community) coordination on 鉱業 sustainability
i: DEA (Department of Environmental Affairs) — under the Ministry of Environment and Tourism, responsible for 環境 policy, impact assessment, and monitoring. Department of Mines — under the Ministry of Minerals and Energy, responsible for 鉱業 safety regulation, licensing, and compliance monitoring. Office of the Ombudsman — investigates complaints against government agencies and public officials.

Debswana Diamond Company — a 50/50 joint venture between the Government of Botswana and De Beers Group, operating the Jwaneng and Orapa mines. Debswana is Botswana's largest private employer and the largest contributor to government revenue. De Beers Group (Anglo American subsidiary) — the global diamond company partnering with Botswana. The 2023 renegotiation of the Debswana agreement gave Botswana a greater share of rough diamond sales.

Ministry of Minerals and Energy — oversees 鉱業 policy and the regulatory framework under the Mines and Minerals Act. BURS (Botswana Unified Revenue Service) — the tax authority responsible for collecting mineral royalties and taxes. Pula Fund — Botswana's sovereign wealth fund (managed by the Bank of Botswana), designed to save diamond revenues for future generations.

National Assembly of Botswana — unicameral parliament with 65 members (57 elected, 8 specially elected). Citizens can contact their MPs through the 議院内閣制 website. Botswana is one of Africa's most stable multiparty democracies, with peaceful transfers of power since independence in 1966. The Ntlo ya Dikgosi (House of Chiefs) — an advisory body representing traditional leaders, with input on matters affecting tribal territories including 鉱業.

BY.s: State-controlled 経済 ($76B GDP) with significant manufacturing (20.3% GDP). Potash 鉱業 (Belaruskali is one of the world's largest producers). Oil refining (Naftan, Mozyr) processes Russian crude. Authoritarian governance under Lukashenko since 1994.

sectors.w: Belarus is the world's third-largest potash producer (Belaruskali). Potash 鉱業 generates massive salt waste (solukhards) that contaminate groundwater and rivers. ...の場合 downstream water contamination data is published alongside potash production revenue, Wが計算可能になる.
bp: International 制裁 (EU, US, UK since 2021) have already weakened the 重複利益 by restricting Belaruskali's market access. ...の場合 sanctioned export revenues fall below 環境 remediation costs, the economic case for continued expansion breaks.

Satellite-based 環境 monitoring (salt waste accumulation, water contamination plumes) published by international organizations. Making 環境 damage visible from space bypasses domestic data suppression and makes W computable.
部分的に破壊可能. Potash 鉱業 inherently generates salt waste. However, solution 鉱業 and backfill techniques can reduce surface waste accumulation and groundwater contamination.
a: Document 環境 violations through secure channels to international 環境 organizations
Report safety concerns through ILO complaint mechanisms
Provide data to international monitoring organizations (Belarusian diaspora networks)
International potash buyers: Demand 環境 certification as condition of purchase (post-制裁)
Apply IFC Performance Standards to any future investment in Belarusian 鉱業
Require independent 環境 audits as part of off-take agreements
International 制裁 bodies: Maintain and strengthen potash export 制裁 until 環境 and governance reforms are implemented
UNECE: Continue 環境 performance reviews of Belarus under the Convention on Environmental Impact Assessment (Espoo Convention)
IndustriALL: Monitor labor rights conditions at Belaruskali
Support maintenance of international 制裁 on Belaruskali until 環境 and governance reforms are implemented
を通じて提唱する： UNECE for transboundary 環境 monitoring of potash 鉱業 impacts
Support Belarusian democratic opposition's platform for 環境 and governance reform
を推進する： international 環境 damage assessment of Soligorsk 鉱業 district
i: Note: Belarus under Lukashenko is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. Independent media, 市民社会, and trade unions face severe repression. There is no effective 内部告発者 protection. After the 2020 protests, thousands were imprisoned and major independent organizations were forcibly liquidated. Safe reporting channels include the Belarusian diaspora network, international trade union federations (IndustriALL Global Union), and international 環境 organizations.

Belaruskali — Belarus's 国有 potash company, one of the world's largest potash producers. Belaruskali operates six mines near Soligorsk. The company has been under EU, US, and UK 制裁 since 2021 following the forced diversion of Ryanair Flight 4978 and repression of democratic opposition. BPC (Belarusian Potash Company) — the former exclusive export trading arm of Belaruskali (sanctioned). Competitors include Nutrien (Canada), Mosaic (US), and K+S (Germany).

Note: Domestic regulatory institutions (Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Protection) are not independent under 権威主義 governance. International mechanisms include: UNECE (United Nations Economic Commission for Europe) — Belarus is party to the Espoo Convention and the Convention on the Protection and Use of Transboundary Watercourses. EU 制裁 framework — Council Regulation (EU) restricting trade with Belaruskali and associated entities.

Note: Belarus has no functioning democratic legislature. The National Assembly (Natsionalnoye Sobranie) is not freely elected. International engagement channels: EU Eastern Partnership framework, OSCE (Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe), United Nations Human Rights Council Universal Periodic Review. The Belarusian democratic opposition in exile (led by Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya) maintains a platform including 環境 governance reform.

CD.s: Resource-rich but impoverished 経済 ($71B GDP). Mining dominates (28.81% of GDP) — world's largest cobalt producer (70%+ global supply), significant copper, coltan, and tin. Conflict minerals remain a governance challenge. Agricultural employment is 60%+ but only 9.6% of GDP.

sectors.w: DRC's cobalt is essential for EV batteries — downstream manufacturers (Tesla, Samsung SDI, CATL) face increasing ESG pressure. ...の場合 supply chain traceability makes mine-level 環境 and labor conditions visible to end consumers, Wが計算可能になる from cobalt prices.
bp: Mandatory supply chain due diligence (EU Battery Regulation, US Dodd-Frank Section 1502) that requires full traceability to mine site. ...の場合 artisanal miners must meet labor and 環境 standards to access global markets, ...における重複利益 unregulated extraction 弱まる.

Blockchain or RMI-style traceability systems linking 鉱山ごとの 環境 and labor data to each cobalt lot. The EU Battery Regulation (effective 2027) will require carbon footprint declarations and due diligence — making W computable per unit of cobalt.
部分的に破壊可能. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes and generates waste. However, formalized artisanal 鉱業 with proper safety and 環境 management can reduce システム依存性 compared to unregulated extraction.
a: Report 環境 or labor violations at mine sites to international monitoring organizations (IPIS, Global Witness)
Document child labor or unsafe working conditions — report through responsible sourcing audit channels (RMI, LBMA)
Report illegal mineral exports to CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales)
Glencore, CMOC (China Moly): 実施する full supply chain traceability to mine site for all DRC cobalt
採用する IRMA (Initiative for Responsible Mining Assurance) certification for industrial operations
に投資する formalization programs for artisanal 鉱業 communities
Ministry of Mines: 施行する 2018 Mining Code 環境 provisions including community development obligations
CEEC: 強化する mineral certification to include 環境 and labor compliance data
Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière (CTCPM): Improve artisanal 鉱業 registration and oversight
あなたの代表者に連絡してください： Député national or Sénateur in the Parlement を要求する enforcement of the 2018 Mining Code 環境 provisions
Support EU Battery Regulation implementation requiring full cobalt supply chain traceability
を通じて提唱する： the African Union for continental responsible 鉱業 standards
を推進する： OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for all minerals sourced from DRC
i: CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales précieuses et semi-précieuses) — the DRC government agency responsible for evaluating, certifying, and controlling mineral exports. SAEMAPE (Service d'Assistance et d'Encadrement du Small Scale Mining) — the agency responsible for regulating artisanal and small-scale 鉱業. IPIS (International Peace Information Service) — an independent research institute mapping 鉱業 sites, armed group presence, and supply chain risks in the DRC. Global Witness — international NGO investigating the links between natural resource exploitation and conflict.

Glencore — operates Mutanda and Katanga Mining (KCC) copper-cobalt mines, among the world's largest. CMOC (China Molybdenum Co.) — operates Tenke Fungurume, one of the world's largest copper-cobalt mines. Entreprise Générale du Cobalt (EGC) — state-backed enterprise intended to monopolize artisanal cobalt purchasing (established 2019). RMI (Responsible Minerals Initiative) — industry-led supply chain due diligence program used by electronics and automotive manufacturers.

Ministry of Mines — oversees 鉱業 policy, licensing, and the 2018 Mining Code (Code Minier), which increased royalty rates and introduced strategic substance classification for cobalt. CTCPM (Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière) — the technical coordination unit for 鉱業 sector planning. ITSCI (ITRI Tin Supply Chain Initiative) — a traceability program for tin, tantalum, and tungsten (3T) minerals from the DRC.

Parlement de la République démocratique du Congo — bicameral legislature consisting of the Assemblée nationale (500 members) and the Sénat (108 members). The DRC is a semi-presidential republic but governance capacity is limited outside major urban centers. EU Battery Regulation (2023/1542) — effective 2027, requires carbon footprint declarations, recycled content minimums, and supply chain due diligence for batteries containing cobalt. OECD Due Diligence Guidance for Responsible Supply Chains of Minerals from Conflict-Affected and High-Risk Areas — the international standard for mineral supply chain responsibility.

CG.s: Oil-dependent 経済 ($15.7B GDP) with 34.74% combined oil and gas rents — one of Sub-Saharan Africa's highest. TotalEnergies and Eni are major operators. Governance challenges with long-ruling president (Sassou Nguesso since 1997). Significant tropical forest cover.

sectors.w: Congo-Brazzaville's oil production is in long-term decline from mature offshore fields. As extraction costs rise and reserves deplete, the 重複利益 between international operators and government revenue 弱まる — diversification becomes necessary.
bp: Redirect oil revenues to economic diversification (forestry, agriculture, services) through transparent budget allocation. ...の場合 non-oil sectors generate more employment and revenue, ...における重複利益 continued extraction from depleting fields breaks.
実施する EITI-standard per-block revenue and 排出 disclosure. Congo joined EITI in 2004 — extending to 環境 data makes W computable per production unit.
破壊不可能 by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report 環境 violations at oil facilities to the Ministry of Environment
Document revenue discrepancies — report to EITI Congo multi-stakeholder group
Report concerns to international operator compliance channels (TotalEnergies, Eni)

SNPC: 策定する economic diversification strategy using petroleum revenues
採用する per-block 排出 reporting standards
Ministry of Hydrocarbons: 強化する 環境 conditions in production sharing contracts
EITI Congo: Expand reporting to include 環境 impact data

Support EITI enhanced reporting with 環境 metrics

Leverage UNFCCC mechanisms for fossil fuel transition support
i: Ministère de l'Environnement, du Développement Durable et du Bassin du Congo — the environment ministry. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company. EITI Congo — the Republic of Congo has been an EITI member since 2004. Note: Whistleblower protections are minimal under current governance.

TotalEnergies — the largest oil operator in Congo-Brazzaville, operating the Moho Nord deepwater project and other fields. Eni — operates significant offshore concessions. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company holding state equity in all production sharing contracts.

Ministère des Hydrocarbures — oversees oil and gas policy and licensing. BEAC (Banque des États de l'Afrique Centrale) — the central bank of the CEMAC monetary zone (shared with Cameroon, CAR, Chad, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon). EITI Congo — publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues.

Note: Congo-Brazzaville under President Sassou Nguesso is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée nationale (151 members) and Sénat (72 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International engagement channels: CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale), African Union, CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) — a partnership between Central African countries and donors to protect tropical forests.

CI.s: Largest 経済 in francophone West Africa ($87.1B GDP). World's largest cocoa producer (~45% of global supply). Agriculture is 15.9% of GDP with cocoa, coffee, and palm oil as main cash crops. Growing oil production (oil=0.7%). Rapid economic growth (~7% annual average).

sectors.w: Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa monoculture has driven 80%+ of original forest loss. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) will require 森林破壊-free supply chains — when per-farm 森林破壊 data is linked to cocoa transactions, Wが計算可能になる.
bp: EU Deforestation Regulation compliance forces traceability to farm level. ...の場合 cocoa buyers must verify zero-森林破壊, the 重複利益 between traders and smallholders in clearing forest for new plantations breaks — agroforestry becomes the viable model.

Mandatory per-farm 環境 data (tree cover, soil carbon, water use) linked to cocoa lot traceability. The Cocoa and Forests Initiative already creates a framework — extending to mandatory data makes W computable from cocoa prices.

部分的に破壊可能. Cocoa monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity compared to native forest. However, cocoa agroforestry systems can maintain significant tree cover and biodiversity — Côte d'Ivoire's National REDD+ Strategy includes agroforestry targets.
a: Report illegal 森林破壊 for cocoa expansion to SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) or OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves)
Document child labor in cocoa production — report to CNS (Comité National de Surveillance des actions de lutte contre le travail des enfants)
Report pesticide misuse — notify ANADER (Agence Nationale d'Appui au Développement Rural)
Cocoa traders (Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam): 実施する full farm-level traceability ahead of EUDR deadline
に投資する cocoa agroforestry systems that maintain tree cover while improving yields
採用する living income reference price commitments for cocoa farmers
CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao): 施行する traceability requirements and link farmgate prices to 環境 compliance
MINEDD (Ministry of Environment): 強化する enforcement against 森林破壊 in classified forests
ANADER: Scale agroforestry extension programs to all cocoa-growing regions

Support EU Deforestation Regulation implementation with adequate transition support for smallholder farmers

を推進する： living income benchmarks in international cocoa pricing through ICCO
i: SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) — the state forestry company responsible for managing classified forests, many of which have been illegally cleared for cocoa. OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves) — manages national parks and protected areas. CNS (Comité National de Surveillance) — the national committee monitoring child labor in cocoa, coordinated with the International Cocoa Initiative (ICI). ANADER — the national rural development support agency providing agricultural extension services.

Major cocoa traders operating in Côte d'Ivoire: Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam International, Sucden. These companies intermediate the majority of Ivorian cocoa exports. Cocoa and Forests Initiative (CFI) — a public-private partnership between cocoa-producing countries (Côte d'Ivoire, Ghana) and leading chocolate companies to end 森林破壊 in cocoa supply chains. The Conseil du Café-Cacao (CCC) — the Ivorian cocoa board that sets farmgate prices and regulates the sector.

CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao) — the government body regulating Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa and coffee sectors, setting minimum farmgate prices, managing quality standards, and overseeing exports. MINEDD (Ministère de l'Environnement et du Développement Durable) — the environment ministry responsible for forest protection, climate policy, and 環境 regulation.

Assemblée nationale de Côte d'Ivoire — unicameral parliament with 255 members. Citizens can contact deputies through the official Assembly website. Sénat — the upper house (99 members, established 2020). ICCO (International Cocoa Organization) — the intergovernmental body coordinating global cocoa policy, headquartered in Abidjan. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR, 2023/1115) — requires EU importers to verify that commodities including cocoa are not linked to 森林破壊 after December 2020.

CL.s: Upper-middle-income 経済 ($330.3B GDP) with massive 鉱業 sector (16.23% of GDP). World's largest copper producer (~27% of global supply) and second-largest lithium producer. Strong institutions and democratic governance. Renewable energy leader in Latin America (solar in Atacama).

sectors.w: Chile's copper and lithium 鉱業 depletes water resources in the Atacama Desert (one of Earth's driest regions). ...の場合 鉱山ごとの water consumption data is published alongside production revenue, Wが計算可能になる from 鉱業 output data.
bp: 実施する water pricing that reflects true scarcity in 鉱業 regions. ...の場合 miners pay the real cost of water extraction in hyper-arid zones, ...における重複利益 water-intensive extraction methods 弱まる — desalination and dry processing become economically preferred.

Mandatory 鉱山ごとの water balance, tailings chemistry, and 排出 disclosure. Chile's SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) already collects 環境 data — making it publicly accessible per mine makes W computable.
部分的に破壊可能. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes. However, dry processing, desalination, and tailings reprocessing can significantly reduce water dependence and waste generation.
a: Report 環境 violations to SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente)
Document water rights violations — report to DGA (Dirección General de Aguas)
Use Chile's 内部告発者 law (Ley 21.015) and 環境 complaint channels (denuncia ambiental) for protected reporting

採用する Science Based Targets for 排出 reduction across 鉱業 operations
実施する ICMM Mining Principles including tailings management standards
SMA: Increase 環境 monitoring frequency and enforce water extraction limits at 鉱業 operations
Sernageomin: 強化する tailings dam safety standards (Chile has 740+ tailings facilities)
Ministry of Mining: 実施する the National Lithium Strategy with binding 環境 standards
あなたの代表者に連絡してください： Diputado or Senador in the Congreso Nacional を要求する mandatory water-neutral 鉱業 operations through desalination requirements
Support the Escazú Agreement on 環境 access rights in Latin America (Chile has not ratified)
Advocate for OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for 鉱業 supply chains
を推進する： international coordination on critical mineral sustainability standards through the Minerals Security Partnership
i: SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) — Chile's 環境 enforcement agency, responsible for monitoring compliance with 環境 permits (RCA — Resolución de Calificación Ambiental). SMA can impose fines up to $8.2M and order facility closure. DGA (Dirección General de Aguas) — the national water authority managing water rights and monitoring extraction. SEA (Servicio de Evaluación Ambiental) — conducts 環境 impact assessments for 鉱業 projects.

CODELCO (Corporación Nacional del Cobre) — Chile's 国有 copper company and the world's largest copper producer. CODELCO operates Chuquicamata, El Teniente, and other major mines. SQM (Sociedad Química y Minera de Chile) — one of the world's largest lithium producers, operating in the Salar de Atacama. Albemarle — US company operating lithium extraction in the Salar de Atacama alongside SQM. BHP, Anglo American — major international copper 鉱業 companies operating in Chile.

Sernageomin (Servicio Nacional de Geología y Minería) — the national geological and 鉱業 service, responsible for mine safety, tailings dam oversight, and geological surveys. Chile has over 740 tailings facilities, the largest inventory in the world. Ministry of Mining — oversees 鉱業 policy including the 2023 National Lithium Strategy, which established state-led lithium development through public-private partnerships.

Congreso Nacional de Chile — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputadas y Diputados (155 members) and the Senado (50 members). Citizens can contact representatives through camara.cl and senado.cl. Chile is an OECD member with strong democratic institutions. Escazú Agreement — the Regional Agreement on Access to Information, Public Participation and Justice in Environmental Matters in Latin America and the Caribbean. Chile signed but has not ratified.

CM.s: Central African 経済 ($53.3B GDP) with oil production (2.94% combined rents) and significant agriculture (18.5% GDP — cocoa, palm oil, timber). Forest cover declining due to logging and agricultural expansion. Governance challenges and Anglophone crisis.

sectors.w: Cameroon's oil production is declining from mature fields. As extraction costs rise, the 重複利益 between operators (Perenco, Addax) and government revenue 弱まる — diversification to sustainable forestry and agriculture becomes economically rational.
bp: Redirect declining oil revenues to sustainable forest management and agricultural diversification. Cameroon's forests are a carbon sink worth more standing than cleared. ...の場合 REDD+ payments exceed marginal oil revenue, ...における重複利益 continued extraction breaks.
実施する EITI-standard per-block 環境 disclosure. Cameroon's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to 環境 data makes W computable.
破壊不可能 by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

Document illegal logging or land seizures — report to international forestry monitoring organizations (Global Forest Watch)

SNH: 策定する post-oil diversification strategy

Support CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) for forest conservation as alternative revenue

を推進する： EITI enhanced reporting
i: MINEPDED (Ministère de l'Environnement, de la Protection de la Nature et du Développement Durable) — Cameroon's environment ministry. SNH (Société Nationale des Hydrocarbures) — the national hydrocarbons company managing state interests in oil and gas. EITI Cameroon — member since 2007. Note: Whistleblower protections are limited.

Perenco — the largest oil producer in Cameroon. Addax Petroleum (Sinopec subsidiary) — operates offshore concessions. SNH manages the state's petroleum interests and holds equity in production sharing contracts.

MINMIDT (Ministère des Mines, de l'Industrie et du Développement Technologique) — oversees 鉱業 and industrial policy. CONAC (Commission Nationale Anti-Corruption) — Cameroon's anti-corruption commission.

Assemblée nationale du Cameroun — 180 members. Sénat — 100 members. Note: Cameroon's democratic institutions are constrained under long-ruling President Paul Biya (in power since 1982). International channels: CEMAC, African Union, CAFI.

CO.s: Major Latin American 経済 ($418.8B GDP) with significant oil (3.42% rents) and coal (0.73% rents) production. Ecopetrol is the largest company. Biodiversity hotspot (most biodiverse country per square kilometer). 2016 peace agreement with FARC opened new governance challenges in former conflict zones.

sectors.w: Colombia's current administration (Petro, 2022-) has announced a policy of no new oil exploration licenses. If maintained, ...における重複利益 fossil fuel expansion breaks as existing fields deplete. Colombia's renewable potential (hydro, solar, wind) provides viable alternatives.
bp: Maintain and implement the no-new-exploration-licenses policy while investing transition revenues in 再生可能エネルギー. ...の場合 energy investment flows shift from fossil to renewable, ...における重複利益 continued exploration breaks.

Mandatory 油田ごとの 排出 disclosure for all Ecopetrol and international operator fields. Colombia's ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) already collects production data — extending to 排出 makes W computable.
破壊不可能 by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

Document community health impacts near oil operations — report to Defensoría del Pueblo

に投資する 再生可能エネルギー in post-conflict zones to provide economic alternatives

Support the Leticia Pact for Amazon conservation as alternative economic model
i: ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales) — Colombia's 環境 licensing authority, responsible for 環境 impact assessment and compliance monitoring for extractive projects. Defensoría del Pueblo — the national human rights ombudsman. Contraloría General de la República — the national audit office overseeing public resource management including oil revenues.

Ecopetrol — Colombia's largest company (88.5% 国有), responsible for most domestic oil production. Ecopetrol has announced energy transition targets but continues significant upstream investment. ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) — manages exploration and production contracts.

MinAmbiente (Ministerio de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sostenible) — Colombia's environment ministry. CREG (Comisión de Regulación de Energía y Gas) — the エネルギー規制当局y commission. CAR (Corporaciones Autónomas Regionales) — regional 環境 authorities.

Congreso de la República de Colombia — bicameral legislature: Cámara de Representantes (188 members) and Senado (108 members). Contact through camara.gov.co and senado.gov.co. Colombia is an OECD member (2020) with strong democratic institutions. The 2016 Peace Agreement (Acuerdo de Paz) created new governance frameworks for former conflict zones that intersect with extractive industry regulation.

CR.s: Upper-middle-income 経済 ($95.4B GDP) with 98%+ renewable electricity generation. No military since 1948. Services-dominant (68.8% GDP). Leader in ecotourism and 環境 policy. Low direct fossil fuel extraction. Main PST exposure through agricultural monoculture (pineapple, banana, palm oil).

sectors.w: Costa Rica's pineapple and banana monoculture uses intensive pesticide application that contaminates waterways. ...の場合 per-farm pesticide runoff and water contamination data is published alongside export revenue, Wが計算可能になる.
bp: 改革する agricultural export incentives to reward biodiversity-positive farming. ...の場合 export tax benefits require 環境 certification, the 重複利益 between monoculture producers and exporters in chemical-intensive production 弱まる.

Mandatory per-farm water quality monitoring downstream of monoculture operations. Costa Rica's AyA (water utility) and SINAC already collect water data — linking to agricultural output makes W computable.
部分的に破壊可能. Tropical monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity. However, Costa Rica's Payment for Environmental Services (PSA) program demonstrates viable alternatives.
a: Report pesticide violations to SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado)
Document water contamination — report to MINAE (Ministry of Environment) or AyA
Use Costa Rica's labor protections and 環境 complaint mechanisms

に投資する organic certification and agroforestry transitions
実施する watershed-level 環境 monitoring
MAG (Ministry of Agriculture): 改革する export incentives to require 環境 certification
MINAE: 強化する pesticide regulations and enforce water quality standards near monoculture areas
SETENA: Require cumulative 環境 impact assessments for monoculture expansion

Support the Escazú Agreement implementation (Costa Rica was an early advocate)
Leverage Costa Rica's 環境 reputation to advance international agricultural sustainability standards
を推進する： green trade provisions in bilateral agreements
i: SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado) — the state phytosanitary service regulating pesticide use. MINAE (Ministerio de Ambiente y Energía) — the environment and energy ministry. AyA (Instituto Costarricense de Acueductos y Alcantarillados) — the national water utility. SETENA (Secretaría Técnica Nacional Ambiental) — conducts 環境 impact assessments.

Costa Rica hosts operations of major fruit multinationals: Del Monte, Dole, Chiquita. CORBANA (Corporación Bananera Nacional) — the national banana corporation coordinating the sector. CANAPEP (Cámara Nacional de Productores y Exportadores de Piña) — the pineapple producers' association.

MAG (Ministerio de Agricultura y Ganadería) — Costa Rica's agriculture ministry. The PSA (Pago por Servicios Ambientales) program — Costa Rica's pioneering payment for 環境 services program, which pays landowners to maintain forest cover, funded partly by a fuel tax.

Asamblea Legislativa de Costa Rica — unicameral parliament with 57 members. Citizens can contact representatives through asamblea.go.cr. Costa Rica is one of Latin America's strongest democracies and a global 環境 leader. The Escazú Agreement — Costa Rica was one of the first signatories of this regional agreement on 環境 access rights.

CU.s: Command 経済 ($107.4B GDP, though estimates vary due to dual currency system). Services-dominant (73.5% GDP, driven by healthcare, education, tourism). Minimal resource extraction. Economic crisis exacerbated by US embargo and collapse of Venezuelan oil subsidies.

sectors.w: Cuba's sugar industry collapsed after Soviet subsidies ended, but remaining agriculture (state farms, cooperatives) relies on monoculture patterns. Cuba's organic farming necessity (post-Soviet fertilizer shortage) demonstrates alternative models. Making soil health data visible breaks system independence.
bp: Expand the urban agriculture and organoponics model that Cuba developed out of necessity. ...の場合 organic production meets domestic food needs more reliably than import-dependent monoculture, ...における重複利益 conventional agriculture breaks.
Publish soil health, water quality, and biodiversity data from Cuba's extensive agricultural research system. Cuba's scientific institutions (INIFAT, ICA) generate data that could make W computable.
部分的に破壊可能. Cuba's experience with organic farming post-1991 shows that システム依存性 on chemical inputs can be dramatically reduced through agroecological methods.
a: Document 環境 degradation through academic research channels
Report through workplace union structures (CTC — Central de Trabajadores de Cuba)
Engage with international agricultural research partnerships
State agricultural enterprises: Scale organoponics and agroecological methods
AZCUBA: Diversify sugar industry toward bioenergy and specialty products
International partners: Support technology transfer for sustainable agriculture
MINAG: Expand support for agroecological transition
CITMA: 強化する 環境 monitoring of agricultural practices
INIFAT: Publish research on agroecological transition outcomes
Support FAO technical cooperation programs for Cuban agricultural sustainability

を推進する： relaxation of US embargo restrictions on agricultural technology transfer
Support South-South cooperation on agroecological methods developed in Cuba
i: Note: Cuba is a 一党独裁国家. Independent 市民社会, media, and labor organizations are not permitted. Reporting channels operate within state structures. CTC (Central de Trabajadores de Cuba) — the state-controlled trade union federation. INIFAT (Instituto Nacional de Investigaciones Fundamentales en Agricultura Tropical) — the national agricultural research institute. CITMA (Ministerio de Ciencia, Tecnología y Medio Ambiente) — the science and environment ministry.

AZCUBA (Empresa Azucarera) — Cuba's state sugar enterprise. State agricultural enterprises and UBPCs (Unidades Básicas de Producción Cooperativa) — cooperatives managing most agricultural production. Cuba's urban agriculture program is one of the world's most successful, producing significant food in cities through organoponics.

MINAG (Ministerio de la Agricultura) — Cuba's agriculture ministry. CITMA — oversees 環境 policy and scientific research. The Asociación Nacional de Agricultores Pequeños (ANAP) — the national small farmers' association, which has been a key partner in agroecological transition.

Note: Cuba's Asamblea Nacional del Poder Popular (605 members) is the legislature but functions within a one-party system. International engagement channels: FAO (Cuba has received recognition for reducing hunger), CELAC (Community of Latin American and Caribbean States), NAM (Non-Aligned Movement). Cuba's agroecological experience is studied globally as a model for post-petrochemical agriculture.

CY.s: Small EU island 経済 ($37.6B GDP) with dominant services sector (76.5% GDP, driven by financial services, shipping, and tourism). No significant resource extraction. Post-2013 banking crisis reforms strengthened financial regulation. Division between Republic of Cyprus and northern territory.

sectors.w: Cyprus's financial services sector has historically facilitated capital flows from Russia and other jurisdictions. EU AML directives and beneficial ownership 透明性 requirements make the welfare impact of opaque financial intermediation computable.
bp: Full implementation of EU Anti-Money Laundering Directives and beneficial ownership 透明性. ...の場合 financial secrecy is eliminated, the 重複利益 between Cypriot service providers and clients seeking opacity 弱まる.

Mandatory publication of Country-by-Country Reporting (CbCR) data for entities registered in Cyprus. EU ATAD and DAC6 frameworks provide the mechanism — making tax planning structures visible breaks system independence.
部分的に破壊可能. Financial intermediation can relocate — but Cyprus's EU加盟国ship, common law heritage, and timezone advantages create switching costs.
a: Report suspicious transactions to MOKAS (Unit for Combating Money Laundering)
Document beneficial ownership concealment — report to CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission)
Use EU Whistleblower Directive protections (transposed 2022)
実施する enhanced due diligence for all high-risk client relationships
採用する full beneficial ownership 透明性 ahead of EU AMLD6 requirements
Integrate ESG criteria into financial advisory services
CySEC: 強化する beneficial ownership verification requirements
CBC: 施行する enhanced AML due diligence for correspondent banking relationships
ICPAC (Institute of Certified Public Accountants): Ensure audit firms enforce 透明性 standards

Support EU AMLD6 negotiations for enhanced 透明性 requirements
Advocate for OECD BEPS Inclusive Framework strengthening
